results.rendered_html: kaṭ-phala m. (for kaṭu-phala, but different in meaning from it), N. of a small tree (found in the north-west of Hindūstān, the aromatic bark and seeds of which are used in medicine; the fruit is eaten; the common name is Kāyaphal),  Suśr.

results.rendered_html:
kaṭphala (kaṭu + phala)
1〉 m. N. eines Baumes mit würziger Frucht und Rinde, welche med. viel gebraucht werden (vulg. kāyaphala),  AK. 2,4,2,21 . 3,4,1,8 .  AINSLIE 2,152 .  SUŚR. 1,138,8 . 141,3 . 142,20 . 2,39,3 . 100,10 . 276,15 . 340,3 . 371,1 . 379,11 . 17 .
— 2〉 f. ˚lā N. verschiedener Pflanzen: Gmelina arborea Roxb. (gambhārī)  RATNAM. _im_ ŚKDR.  verschiedene Species von Solanum (bṛhatī, kākamācī, vārtākī), = devadālī (vgl. kaṇṭaphalā) und mṛgervāru  RĀJAN. _im_ ŚKDR.
